skill.name: videocut
skill.description: Execute timestamp-driven talking-head video edits with FFmpeg, then re-transcribe with FunASR and generate subtitles.
skill.summary: Cut confirmed speech mistakes from videos using timestamp-based FFmpeg edits.
skill.author: Ceeon

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and filesystem patterns, but review shows most are markdown examples or legitimate FFmpeg/FunASR workflow instructions. No evidence found of malware, credential theft, network exfiltration, or prompt-injection override text. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it guides local command execution and temporary/cache file writes.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate Local Media Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill instructs the assistant to run FFmpeg and includes Python subprocess.run examples for FFmpeg. This is expected for video cutting, but it can process user-selected files and should be limited to trusted local media paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is explicit and semantically required by the skill. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands target FFmpeg and local transcription workflows.

medium_findings.title: Temporary Files and Local Model Cache Usage
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The guidance writes WAV segments under /tmp and documents FunASR model downloads to a hidden home cache. These actions are normal for transcription, but they can leave media-derived files or large model artifacts on disk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths and cache location are directly documented. The guidance removes segment files in one example, but the workflow still creates local artifacts.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Scope Maintenance File References
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. Comment blocks tell maintainers to update ../README.md and /CLAUDE.md when files change. This is not part of the video workflow, but an agent should avoid modifying parent or root configuration files unless the user explicitly asks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are real but appear to be maintainer notes, not runtime instructions. They still cross normal skill boundaries and deserve a warning.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Many external_commands alerts are inline code spans or fenced markdown examples, not Ruby backtick execution in runnable source code. They document commands, file names, and example text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are markdown documentation, not executable Ruby code. No Ruby source file or evaluated backtick expression was found.

low_findings.title: Unicode and Entropy Alerts Caused by Chinese Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The unicode escape and high entropy alerts are explained by Chinese-language markdown content and one normal Unicode range check in a tokenizer example. No encrypted payload or obfuscated executable content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious entropy aligns with readable non-English prose. The unicode escape is part of a transparent Chinese character tokenizer example.

dangerous_patterns.title: FFmpeg Execution on Local Media
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow relies on FFmpeg commands and subprocess.run examples. This is appropriate for the skill, but users should review input and output paths before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly present and central to the skill. It is dangerous only if paths or media files are untrusted.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Temporary Audio Artifacts
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples create temporary WAV files and remove them later. Users should confirm cleanup and avoid placing sensitive media in shared temporary directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The temporary paths are directly shown. The examples include cleanup in one workflow, which reduces but does not remove the risk.

content.value_statement: Manual speech cleanup is slow when mistakes, filler words, and silences need precise cuts. This skill turns confirmed timestamp tasks into FFmpeg edits, verification transcriptions, and subtitle output.
actual_capabilities: Reads confirmed deletion tasks from a timestamp-based TodoList.
Builds FFmpeg trim and concat guidance for precise video cuts.
Supports versioned output names such as edited video, transcript, and SRT files.
Uses FunASR re-transcription guidance to verify remaining speech mistakes.
Documents methods for filler words, silence, and micro-stumble detection.
limitations: Requires local FFmpeg and FunASR setup before use.
Depends on accurate timestamps and explicit user confirmation.
Optimized for Chinese talking-head speech workflows.
Does not provide a visual editing interface.

use_cases.title: Clean Course Recording Mistakes
use_cases.description: Remove repeated phrases, false starts, and long silences from instructional videos after reviewing a timestamped task list.

use_cases.title: Prepare Short Talking-Head Clips
use_cases.description: Create tighter social clips by applying confirmed FFmpeg cuts and generating matching subtitles.

use_cases.title: Verify Transcript-Based Edits
use_cases.description: Re-transcribe edited videos to check whether speech mistakes remain before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Begin a Confirmed Cut
prompt_templates.scenario: Start from an approved deletion checklist.
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the confirmed video cut using this deletion TodoList. Keep unchecked items, create the next versioned video, and generate subtitles.

prompt_templates.title: Keep Selected Silences
prompt_templates.scenario: Remove most issues but preserve chosen pauses.
prompt_templates.prompt: Cut all checked speech mistakes and filler words, but keep silence items 3 and 5. Show the planned retained time ranges before running FFmpeg.

prompt_templates.title: Recheck the Edited Version
prompt_templates.prompt: Re-transcribe the edited video with FunASR and review whether any mouth mistakes, filler words, or long silences remain.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Timestamp Repair
prompt_templates.scenario: Fix difficult boundaries before another cut pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these uncertain deletion boundaries. Use token timestamps, preserve the correct spoken version, and propose exact start and end times for the next FFmpeg pass.

output_examples.input: A confirmed checklist removes three speech mistakes and two long pauses from a course video.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a versioned edited video plan, confirms retained segments, runs the cut workflow, and produces subtitle output.

output_examples.input: The edited video still contains a short false start at the beginning.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the remaining micro-stumble, proposes a precise timestamp range, and prepares another versioned edit pass.

output_examples.input: A creator wants to keep two natural pauses while removing filler words.
output_examples.output: The assistant keeps the selected pauses, removes confirmed filler ranges, and explains the final media and subtitle files.

best_practices: Confirm every deletion item before running FFmpeg.
Use timestamp ranges instead of searching spoken text.
Review the re-transcription before publishing the final video.
anti_patterns: Do not run cuts from an unchecked or ambiguous task list.
Do not use text search when exact timestamps are available.
Do not process sensitive media in shared temporary directories.

faq.question: What tools does this skill expect?
faq.answer: It expects FFmpeg for media editing and FunASR for transcription and verification.

faq.question: Can it create subtitles?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow produces SRT subtitles after the edited video passes review.

faq.question: Does it decide what to delete automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to execute confirmed deletion tasks from a reviewed TodoList.

faq.question: What language is it optimized for?
faq.answer: The guidance is optimized for Chinese speech, FunASR timestamps, and Chinese filler word patterns.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill guides local FFmpeg execution and temporary file writes. These are legitimate but require trusted paths and user review.

faq.question: Can it replace a full video editor?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on timestamp-driven speech cleanup, not timeline design, effects, or manual visual editing.
